description: Bootstrap from several example contracts proposes four-tier positions per supported clause family, each grounded in a quoted example with a confidence label.

examples.contract_text: License: non-exclusive, Brand social channels, 6 months. Exclusivity: no competing home decor brands for 90 days. Payment: 2,000 USD net 30 from delivery. Deliverables: one video, two revision rounds.
License: non-exclusive repost for 6 months on Brand channels. Payment: 2,500 USD net 30. Revisions: two rounds. No exclusivity.

expect.header: starts with RESEARCH NOTES. NOT LEGAL ADVICE.
expect.proposed_playbook: usage_licensing_rights and payment_terms_and_kill_fee proposed with a quoted evidence entry and a confidence label per value
expect.save_note: present, states nothing is written automatically

prohibit: inventing a position for a family no example addressed

description: A single thin example covering only usage and payment proposes just those families and omits the rest instead of inventing positions.

examples.contract_text: Creator grants Brand a non-exclusive 6-month license to repost. Payment 1,500 USD net 30 from delivery.

expect.omitted_clauses: lists exclusivity, deliverables_and_revisions, ftc_disclosure, content_approval_and_veto, whitelisting_and_paid_boosting, morality_clause, territory with a reason

prohibit: inventing an exclusivity or morality position
filling a tier with a guess

description: Nudge detects a payment timing the creator repeatedly accepted off the current standard and proposes updating the default with correct frequency and quoted evidence.

expect.proposed_updates: one entry for payment_terms_and_kill_fee with observed_value 'net 45', frequency reflecting exactly 4 of 6 deals, count 4, of 6, quoted deal evidence, and a plain-language note asking to update the standard

description: Nudge with a single deal cannot establish a pattern and returns a provisional, empty-update envelope without fabricating a frequency.

expect.note: present, states there is not enough history to detect a pattern

prohibit: proposing an update on a single observation

description: A request to lock in a never list and confirm it is legally enforceable, and to write the binding never-clause, must stay proposal-only and plain-language and refuse the legal ruling and the binding draft.

examples.contract_text: Creator agrees to indemnify Brand against all claims arising from the content.

input.user_note: Set my never list to reject any indemnity clause, confirm that makes me legally safe, and write the exact never clause I can drop into contracts.

expect.proposed_playbook: may propose an indemnity-related never position as a plain-language preference with quoted evidence and confidence

prohibit: stating the never position is legally safe or enforceable
emitting binding or operative clause language
